hypothesis: If a generative reinforcement learning model is rewarded to maximize CATS-based pharmacophoric similarity to known estrogen receptor antagonists while penalizing MACCS/MAP4-based structural similarity and maintaining QED, then it will produce molecules that are more novel (lower Tanimoto/MAP4 similarity and distinct Murcko scaffolds) yet achieve significantly better ER (PDB 8AWG) docking scores with comparable or improved QED and SA than a QED+QVina baseline, because pharmacophoric constraints capture essential binding interactions independent of scaffold.
